title: Category update parity hardening, rollback lock, and boundary closure

empty_name_contract.reason: Category create and tag rename already reject empty trimmed names; allowing category update to write an empty string is a data-quality gap, not a useful contract.

success_invariants: Successful category update changes only the targeted Categories row fields provided by the request
No file, attachment, import/export, cleanup, server, migration, Caddy, systemd, frontend default, or Pi side effect
failure_invariants: Duplicate category name leaves Categories unchanged
Trimmed empty category name leaves Categories unchanged
Disabled Go category-write flag leaves Categories unchanged

boundary_lock.live_owner: Python remains live category update owner
blocked_scope: Tag delete or merge Go ownership

allowed_next_step.title: File / attachment ownership gate
allowed_next_step.scope: Start with plan-only file ownership inventory for uploads, attachments, cleanup, export/import, and backup/log surfaces. Do not implement file routes, Caddy/systemd changes, production DB writes, Pi deploy, or Python removal without a new explicit gate.
